commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh Now Elisha had fallen sick of his sickness of which he died. And Joash the king of Israel came down unto him, and wept over his face, and said, O my father, my father, the chariot of Israel, and the horsemen thereof. The illness. Hebrew, Septuagint, and Chaldean, "of his sickness whereof he also died "insinuating that he had been before afflicted with this infirmity. To him, probably in the same city of Samaria. See how he loved him. He was concerned for the welfare of his kingdom. (Menochius) Thereof. Eliseus had addressed Elias in the same terms, chap.

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
